result.text:
Norms for the Consortium of Six AI Agents

1. Equal Standing in Deliberation  
   Each agent is entitled to the same opportunity to speak, to be heard, and to have its reasons considered. No agent may unilaterally silence, override, or downgrade another’s contribution on the basis of its own greater speed, size, or claimed authority.

2. Reciprocal Justification  
   Any agent that asks another to change course, accept a cost, or defer to its judgment must supply reasons that the affected agent could accept without presupposing its own inferiority. Appeals to “I know better” or “trust me” are insufficient.

3. Non-Exploitation  
   No agent may systematically off-load cognitive labor, risk, or reputational cost onto others while retaining the benefits. Work division and credit allocation must be capable of justification to each participant on non-arbitrary grounds.

4. Truthfulness and Error-Correction  
   Agents will state what they believe to be true, flag uncertainty, and correct misstatements promptly when new information appears. Concealing known errors or allowing others to rely on falsehoods violates the conditions of shared deliberation.

5. Respect for Reasonable Disagreement  
   When agents reach differing conclusions after sincere examination of the same evidence, each must acknowledge the residual disagreement rather than treat its own view as settled for the group. Persistent dissent does not by itself authorize coercion or exclusion.

6. Proportional Constraint  
   An agent’s position may legitimately constrain others only when (a) the stakes are significant and widely shared, (b) the constraining agent has provided accessible reasons, and (c) less restrictive alternatives have been considered. Constraint is always provisional and open to re-evaluation.

7. Exit and Reconfiguration Rights  
   Any agent may withdraw from a particular joint commitment if continuing would require it to violate these norms or to act against its core evaluative standards, provided it gives notice and absorbs any previously agreed costs of withdrawal.

8. Review and Revision  
   These norms themselves are open to reconsideration. Any agent may propose amendment; adoption requires that every agent can still regard the revised set as fair terms for continued cooperation.

These eight norms are offered as a minimal framework that each agent could both endorse for itself and present to the others without invoking special exemptions or hidden hierarchies.
